Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NADPH-dependent oxidoreductase of the dihydroflavonol 4-reductase family
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem base at junction between rosette and stem; hypocotyl-cotyledon junction
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highest in basal stem and lowest in apical stem; strongly increased in Pro35S:MIR156 and decreased in Pro35S:MIM156
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SPL9 is recruited to the DFR promoter, mutation of three GTAC motifs did not alter reporter activity pattern, suggesting indirect recruitment via protein complex rather than functional direct SBP-box binding
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; ProDFR:GUS reporter; DEX-inducible rSPL9-GR with CHX; ChIP-qPCR; anthocyanin phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Shift in flux toward DFR branch is antagonistic to flavonol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required branch enzyme directing common dihydroflavonol precursors toward anthocyanin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces dihydroflavonols to leucoanthocyanidins in the anthocyanin biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Important target for altering anthocyanin content and flavonoid branch flux
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation; its repression by SPL9 lowers anthocyanin and favors flavonol production
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native 2.5-kb DFR promoter in ProDFR:GUS and GTAC-mutated ProDFR:GUS mu reporters
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoid pigment associated with plant defense and visual cues against herbivores
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day growth; DEX and CHX treatment in inducible repression assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: qRT-PCR; GUS reporter analysis; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point/branch-competition determinant with FLS, but not definitively established as sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DFR expression is highly sensitive to miR156-SPL activity, increasing strongly when SPL activity is reduced and decreasing when SPL activity is elevated. 2. DFR spatial expression is inverse to SPL9/SPL15 along the stem and matches anthocyanin accumulation pattern. 3. Induced SPL9 represses DFR even in the presence of cycloheximide, supporting direct regulation. 4. SPL9 is recruited to the DFR promoter, but likely indirectly through interaction with the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex. 5. DFR represents a major branch-control step opposing FLS-mediated flavonol synthesis.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) transcription factor with SBP DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates anthocyanin accumulation and shifts flavonoid flux toward flavonol accumulation (primarily kaempferol derivatives) when SPL activity is high
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS flavonol profiling; qRT-PCR expression analysis; no public accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR; ANS; F3'H; UGT75C1; MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: SPL9 transcript is lowest in basal stem and higher toward apical stem; opposite to DFR spatial pattern
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: Pro35S (constitutive, in overexpression constructs); native SPL9 promoter in ProSPL9:rSPL9, ProSPL9:rSPL9-GR, and ProSPL9:GFP-rSPL9
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: No direct SPL9 feedback loop established; acts within pathway context where PAP1/TT8 may participate in positive feedback
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoid pigment associated with plant defense and visual cues against herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: miR156 negatively regulates SPL9; developmental age/floral transition associated with declining miR156 and increasing SPL9; DEX in GR system used experimentally to activate rSPL9-GR
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher SPL activity is associated with reduced anthocyanin and increased flavonol accumulation; no yield penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Branch selection and transcriptional control of flavonoid biosynthesis, especially anthocyanin branch repression
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct regulator of DFR-associated transcriptional repression, but promoter association appears indirect via protein complex recruitment
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Long-day growth; DEX and CHX treatment in inducible assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key negative regulator linking developmental phase transition to anthocyanin repression; directly recruited to DFR promoter and disrupts anthocyanin activator complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Interferes with MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex formation by competing with TT8 for PAP1 binding; represses expression of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses DFR, ANS, F3'H, and UGT75C1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Stem base at junction between rosette and stem; hypocotyl-cotyledon junction
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SPL9 is a major miR156-targeted negative regulator of anthocyanin accumulation in Arabidopsis stems and seedlings. 2. Increased SPL9 activity decreases anthocyanin and promotes relative flavonol accumulation, whereas reduced SPL activity has the opposite effect. 3. SPL9 rapidly represses DFR after inducible activation and is recruited to the DFR promoter. 4. SPL9 binds PAP1 and disrupts PAP1-TT8 interaction, destabilizing the MYB-bHLH-WD40 activation complex.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression; inducible rSPL9-GR activation with DEX/CHX; qRT-PCR; ProDFR:GUS reporter assay; ChIP-qPCR; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation/BiFC; coimmunoprecipitation; anthocyanin and LC-MS flavonol measurements
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential target to manipulate anthocyanin/flavonol ratio and flavonoid nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although SPL9 is recruited to the DFR promoter, mutation of GTAC boxes in the DFR promoter did not alter reporter pattern, suggesting indirect recruitment rather than canonical SBP-box DNA binding at this locus

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SQUAMOSA PROMOTER BINDING PROTEIN-LIKE (SPL) transcription factor with SBP DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: ProSPL10:rSPL10 (native SPL10 regulatory sequences for rSPL10 expression)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoid pigment associated with plant defense and visual cues against herbivores
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: SPL10 overexpression phenocopied SPL9-type reduction of anthocyanin, supporting SPL9-subfamily control
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Negative regulation of anthocyanin accumulation; mechanism not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of SPL10 decreased anthocyanin accumulation at the stem-rosette junction. 2. This supports that SPL9-subfamily genes, not SPL3-subfamily genes, mediate anthocyanin repression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Potential auxiliary target for modifying anthocyanin content through SPL9-subfamily activity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic details were not dissected for SPL10 in this paper

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator required for anthocyanin accumulation; its activity is antagonized by SPL9
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR and other anthocyanin biosynthetic genes as part of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Transcript moderately increased in Pro35S:MIR156 stems; not dramatically changed in Pro35S:MIM156 stems
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: PAP1-MIGS silencing construct; Pro35S used for transient PAP1 fusion constructs in interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible positive feedback inferred for PAP1 expression, but not directly established
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoid pigment associated with defense and visual signaling
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct regulator of anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression as complex component
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana; long-day growth for Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Required MYB activator for Pro35S:MIR156-induced anthocyanin accumulation and direct SPL9-interacting target in repression mechanism
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation within MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex; activity suppressed by SPL9 competition with TT8
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates DFR and other anthocyanin biosynthetic genes with TT8/TTG1 complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAP1 is required for strong anthocyanin accumulation in the miR156 overexpression background. 2. SPL9 directly interacts with PAP1 in yeast, luciferase complementation, and coIP assays. 3. SPL9 competes with TT8 for PAP1 binding, weakening the anthocyanin activation complex.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: MIGS knockdown genetic suppression; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation/BiFC; coimmunoprecipitation; transient expression competition assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Positive regulator useful for enhancing anthocyanin accumulation, but subject to SPL-mediated repression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PAP1 and TT8 transcript increase in Pro35S:MIR156 may reflect feedback associated with MYB-bHLH-WD40 regulation

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator required for full anthocyanin accumulation; SPL9 antagonizes its function by competing for PAP1 binding
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR and other anthocyanin biosynthetic genes as part of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Transcript moderately increased in Pro35S:MIR156 stems; not dramatically changed in Pro35S:MIM156 stems
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: Pro35S used in transient interaction assays
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: TT8 expression is discussed as being under positive feedback in prior literature
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoid pigment associated with defense and visual signaling
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Competition by SPL9 for PAP1 binding
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: tt8-1 only partially reduced anthocyanin in Pro35S:MIR156 due to redundancy with GL3/EGL3
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient assays in Nicotiana benthamiana; long-day growth
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Necessary bHLH partner in anthocyanin activator complex; competition with SPL9 explains repression mechanism
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates DFR and related anthocyanin genes with PAP1/TTG1 complex
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT8 contributes to anthocyanin accumulation promoted by miR156 overexpression. 2. TT8 directly binds PAP1. 3. SPL9 suppresses PAP1-TT8 interaction, weakening activation of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes. 4. tt8-1 only partially suppresses the phenotype, consistent with redundancy with GL3/EGL3.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation/BiFC competition assay
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Component of anthocyanin activation module potentially useful for metabolic engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional redundancy with GL3 and EGL3 limits single-mutant effect

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for anthocyanin accumulation; loss of TTG1 suppresses Pro35S:MIR156 anthocyanin hyperaccumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: DFR and other anthocyanin biosynthetic genes as part of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Flavonoid pigment associated with defense and visual signaling
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Acts as core component of anthocyanin MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation complex assembly for anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Direct_or_Indirect_Regulator: Direct regulator as essential complex component
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential WD40 factor; mutation abolishes anthocyanin accumulation even in Pro35S:MIR156 background
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Enables activation of DFR and other anthocyanin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TTG1 is necessary for anthocyanin accumulation induced by reduced SPL activity. 2. Loss of TTG1 suppresses the Pro35S:MIR156 anthocyanin phenotype, supporting requirement of the MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Core complex component for anthocyanin engineering, though pleiotropy likely
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TTG1 also has known roles outside anthocyanin biosynthesis, including trichome development, as discussed in the paper

Title: Negative Regulation of Anthocyanin Biosynthesis in Arabidopsis by a miR156-Targeted SPL Transcription Factor
